create_pocket_factory.configuration.entr: Choose how the entrance and a held bound entrance render the factory preview.
create_pocket_factory.configuration.rota: When disabled, the entrance miniature or core model remains still.
create_pocket_factory.configuration.rend: When enabled, both the miniature and the large projection render Create block entity parts. When disabled, both projection sizes skip the BER path entirely and only keep cached static block models.

When enabled, the large projection also renders animated Create block entity parts such as shafts, chain wheels, and steam engine linkages. When disabled, those parts remain visible in a static pose to keep the projection lighter while preserving the silhouette.
create_pocket_factory.configuration.show: When enabled, nearby linked blocks in view occasionally emit a small upward particle as a marker.
tooltip.create_pocket_factory.pocket_fac: Unbound factory: one will be assigned after placement.
tooltip.create_pocket_factory.linked_chu: Unbound factory: this will become a linked chute endpoint in phase 6.
tooltip.create_pocket_factory.pocket_fac: Right-click to open a portable Pocket Factory panel.
It can list every factory and teleport you into any Pocket Factory from anywhere.
It can also return you quickly from the factory dimension, but it no longer restores lost entrances.
Hold and right-click Pocket Factory Block A to trigger factory expansion.
Horizontal expansion consumes 1 core; vertical expansion is fixed at 1 block per use.
Hold and right-click a normal Item Vault to convert the whole multiblock into a Linked Item Vault.
Hold and right-click a normal Fluid Tank to convert the whole multiblock into a Linked Fluid Tank.
Hold and right-click two normal vertical chutes to create a new linked chute binding.
Hold and right-click two normal clutches to create a new linked clutch binding.
Hold and right-click two normal mechanical pumps to create a new linked pump binding.
debug.create_pocket_factory.menu.title: Pocket Factory Management Panel
debug.create_pocket_factory.menu.empty: There are no registered factories yet.
debug.create_pocket_factory.menu.require: You must hold the Super Pocket Factory Core to use the portable teleport commands.
debug.create_pocket_factory.menu.factory: Factory ID %s still has a valid entrance and does not need recovery.
debug.create_pocket_factory.menu.recover: Granted a recovery entrance item for Factory ID %s.
create_pocket_factory.ponder.tag.pocket_: Tools and linked devices for expanding a factory space and moving items, fluids, and rotation across dimensions
create_pocket_factory.ponder.pocket_fact: Extending a Pocket Factory with the Core
The Pocket Factory Core is used to expand the factory itself.
Right-clicking a white floor block can extend the factory downward.
Right-clicking a white wall block can extend the factory sideways.
Horizontal expansion consumes a core. Vertical expansion grows one block at a time.
Creating Linked Blocks with the Core
The core can also bind compatible Create blocks into linked variants.
Linked blocks cannot be crafted directly. They are created by binding two matching endpoints.
Placing the entrance binds it to a dedicated Pocket Factory.
Right-click the entrance to enter the factory. Leaving it returns you to your recorded location whenever possible.
Even if the entrance is broken, placing its bound entrance again will restore access to the same factory.
Inside the Pocket Factory, right-clicking any gray block returns you to the outside world.
Any Pocket Factory Block B surface can be used as an exit.
If you need to place blocks on the gray exit surface, hold Shift while right-clicking.
create_pocket_factory.ponder.linked_chut: Use a Wrench on the lower chutes to turn them into windowed chutes.
With ordinary chutes, items simply fall straight down into the vault below.
Use the core on the upper two chutes to bind them into a linked pair.
After binding, items entering the left upper chute are sent to the other column and fall into the second vault.
One chute column can sit directly on an Encased Fan, while the other is lowered by one block and capped with a windowed chute.
After binding the chute above the fan to the middle chute of the opposite column, the airflow crosses over and bursts out of the top windowed chute on the other side.
create_pocket_factory.ponder.linked_pump: Local and Cross Transfer with Linked Mechanical Pumps
This scene only keeps the fluids at each pump tail, showing water and lava as the two local inputs.
In the ordinary setup, the front output stays the same: the upper pump outputs water and the lower pump outputs lava.
After binding the two pumps with the core, linked mechanical pumps swap which tail fluid each side uses.
Once the old outputs are cleared and placed again, the upper front becomes lava while the lower front becomes water.
create_pocket_factory.ponder.linked_clut: Straight and Cross Transfer with Linked Clutches
This linked pair can be placed directly into two lines: the upper line is powered by a Creative Motor, while the lower line has no rotational input of its own.
When both sides share the same redstone state, linked clutches stay in straight-through mode and keep power on the local line.
When only one side is powered, they switch into cross mode and redirect the powered line's speed and stress to the opposite output.
That lets linked clutches switch where power exits: local when the redstone states match, crossed when they differ.
